title: Create firmware board capability matrix
description: Why this exists: we need to decide what we implement as reference targets and what the dev partner can safely scale out. Board support differs across radio chips, displays, buttons, GNSS, PMICs, BLE, serial, flash, and MCU families. What needs to be done: build a capability matrix for target families and representative boards: LilyGO T-Beam/T-Beam Supreme/LoRa32, Heltec LoRa 32 V2/V3, RAK4631/RAK11310/RAK3172, T-Echo-like nRF52840 boards, STM32WL boards, gateway/bridge boards, and headless modules. Track MCU, radio, display controller, input controls, GNSS source, time source, PMIC/battery telemetry, BLE/USB/UART, external flash/storage, RAM/flash constraints, Zephyr board support status, and whether we or partner should own the port. Acceptance criteria: matrix is committed in project docs or Bead design field; at least four reference profiles are selected: display+buttons+GNSS, display+buttons without GNSS, headless/module, and gateway/bridge; partner-owned long-tail targets are clearly identified; two review passes before closure.
notes:
2026-07-01 no-hardware implementation: added docs/firmware-board-capability-matrix.md as the committed firmware board capability matrix and partner-handoff baseline. The matrix tracks MCU/memory, radio, display, inputs, GNSS/time, PMIC/battery, local interfaces, storage, Zephyr status, and ownership across T-Echo, Heltec V3, T-Deck, RAK4631, R1 Neo, T1000-E, STM32WL class, ESP32/SX127x class, T-Beam Supreme, RAK11310/RP2040 class, and Linux/simulator/gateway targets. Selected four LICHEN-owned reference profiles: display+buttons+GNSS (T-Echo), display+buttons without GNSS (Heltec WiFi LoRa 32 V3), headless/module (RAK4631 with R1 Neo sibling), and gateway/bridge (native_sim/qemu_x86, RAK4631 serial bridge, nRF52840 DK BLE shell). Partner-owned long-tail targets are explicitly listed.

Review evidence: initial independent reviews by McClintock and Kant found no P0s. McClintock reported P2s that T-Echo e-ink was overstated as a modeled display node and nRF52840 DK was described as external-SX1262 gateway despite current config being BLE/CoAP shell; fixed both. Kant reported P2 that T1000-E LR1110/GNSS wording ignored app overlays enabling lora0/gnss0; fixed by distinguishing board DTS default from app-overlay enablement and pending validation. Final reviews by Curie and Helmholtz: Curie found no P0/P1/P2 and confirmed acceptance criteria; Helmholtz found P2 that R1 Neo PMIC/battery wording implied telemetry was modeled; fixed to state no battery0/pmic0 telemetry node and HAL battery/PMIC disabled. The only remaining reviewer P1 was the expected pre-commit untracked-doc state, resolved by the implementation commit.

Verification: git diff --check PASS after final edits. No EC2 or Zephyr build required for doc-only matrix work. Residual hardware risks remain called out in the document; physical validation stays in separate hardware Beads.
close_reason: Completed: committed docs/firmware-board-capability-matrix.md with four selected reference profiles, partner-owned long-tail targets, no-hardware evidence, review outcomes, and git diff --check pass.
